note: Replicate is a developer-only API (no UI/config/permissions) exposing one service and four events. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read the source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, which service do I use to duplicate a content entity in code, and which method clones AND saves it versus returning an unsaved clone?
evals.expected_output: The replicate.replicator service (Drupal\replicate\Replicator). replicateEntity()/replicateByEntityId() clone and save; cloneEntity()/cloneByEntityId() return an unsaved clone.

evals.prompt: In PHP, replicate (clone and save) node 1 on a Drupal 11 site using the Replicate module. Show the code.
evals.expected_output: Get \Drupal::service('replicate.replicator') and call replicateByEntityId('node', 1) (or load the node and call replicateEntity($node)).

evals.prompt: Using the Replicate module, I want to control how one specific field type is duplicated when an entity is cloned. Which event do I subscribe to and what does the listener receive?
evals.expected_output: Subscribe to replicate__entity_field__{field_type} (built via ReplicatorEvents::replicateEntityField()); the listener gets a ReplicateEntityFieldEvent with getFieldItemList() (the field on the clone) and getEntity() (the clone).

evals.prompt: With the Replicate module, which event lets me change how a whole entity of a given type is replicated (for example append ' [clone]' to every cloned node title), and what class is dispatched?
evals.expected_output: Subscribe to replicate__entity__{entity_type_id} (ReplicateEntityEvent, carries the original) or replicate__alter (ReplicateAlterEvent, carries clone + original) to adjust the whole cloned entity.

evals.prompt: When Replicate clones an entity, are entity-reference fields deep-cloned or shallow by default, and how do I make a referenced entity be deep-cloned instead?
evals.expected_output: By default the clone is shallow (createDuplicate copies reference values, so targets are shared). To deep-clone, subscribe to the reference field's replicate__entity_field__ event and replace each target with a fresh clone by re-invoking replicate.replicator (recursion), as the Layout Builder inline-block subscriber does.

evals.prompt: Does the Replicate module provide an admin UI, configuration form, or permissions for cloning content, or is it a developer API?
evals.expected_output: It is a developer-only API: no admin UI, no configuration, no permissions. Cloning is done in code via the replicate.replicator service; UI modules like Quick Node Clone / Entity Clone build on this pattern.

evals.prompt: With Replicate, which event fires after a replicated entity has been saved so I can react (log, queue, notify), and which constant names it?
evals.expected_output: The replicate__after_save event (ReplicatorEvents::AFTER_SAVE), dispatched as an AfterSaveEvent whose getEntity() is the saved clone; it only fires from the replicate* (save) methods.
